name: Extra-Virgin Olive Oil
summary: The cornerstone fat of the Mediterranean diet — mostly monounsaturated fat plus protective polyphenols, and the oil with the strongest clinical-trial evidence for heart health.
superfoodReason: It is the fat at the centre of the most rigorously trial-tested dietary pattern (PREDIMED), pairing monounsaturated fat with polyphenols that carry an approved health claim for protecting blood cholesterol from oxidation.

benefits.claim: Lowers cardiovascular risk as the core fat of the Mediterranean diet
benefits.mechanism: Extra-virgin olive oil is rich in the monounsaturated fat oleic acid and in polyphenols. In the PREDIMED randomized trial of 7,447 people at high cardiovascular risk, a Mediterranean diet supplemented with extra-virgin olive oil reduced the rate of major cardiovascular events (heart attack, stroke, or cardiovascular death) compared with a control low-fat diet — one of the strongest pieces of evidence that a specific fat can protect the heart.

citations.title: Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease with a Mediterranean Diet Supplemented with Extra-Virgin Olive Oil or Nuts (Estruch et al., N Engl J Med 2018)
Exploring the Benefits of Extra Virgin Olive Oil on Cardiovascular Health Enhancement and Disease Prevention: A Systematic Review (Nutrients 2025)

benefits.claim: Its polyphenols protect blood cholesterol from oxidation
benefits.mechanism: The phenolic compounds in virgin olive oil — hydroxytyrosol and its derivatives — help shield LDL cholesterol from oxidative damage, a step thought to drive artery-clogging plaque. In the EUROLIVE randomized crossover trial, higher-polyphenol olive oil improved the lipid profile and reduced markers of oxidative damage more than low-polyphenol oil. The effect is strong enough that European regulators granted olive-oil polyphenols an approved health claim.

citations.title: The effect of polyphenols in olive oil on heart disease risk factors: a randomized trial (Covas et al., EUROLIVE Study, Ann Intern Med 2006)

surprisingFacts.text: The peppery sting good extra-virgin olive oil leaves at the back of your throat comes from oleocanthal — a polyphenol that acts on the same anti-inflammatory pathway as ibuprofen, which is why researchers describe it as a natural anti-inflammatory.

cautions.effect: It's almost pure fat, so the only real ceiling is calories — roughly 120 kcal a tablespoon. Nothing about it is toxic in culinary amounts.

affiliateSlots.label: Shop extra-virgin olive oil on Amazon
